user_query: Our village’s fields were devastated, and I need to judge which official guidance we can actually use right now. Please: 1) Perform search engine queries to find and download at least five official guidance documents on post-disaster agricultural recovery: at least three from the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO; source domain should contain "fao.org") and at least two from a national agriculture ministry or agricultural extension service (source domain should contain one of "gov", "ministry", or "agric"). Use search terms that combine the topics in input/criteria.json (e.g., soil rehabilitation, drought-resistant, cropping calendar). Save each file under data/raw/ with unique, descriptive filenames. 2) Write and run a command-line script (any language) that reads input/criteria.json and processes all files under data/raw/. For each document, extract: source_domain, infer source_org as "FAO" or "Government/Extension" based on domain patterns, title (best-effort), publication_year (best-effort; null if absent), file_type (pdf or html), matched_keywords (list of keywords from criteria.json found in the document), and matched_section_terms (list based on criteria.json). Use the scoring_rules in input/criteria.json to compute a numeric score and an "actionable" vs "reference-only" classification. 3) Write these outputs: - output/docs_metadata.json: an array of objects, each with fields doc_id, source_org, source_domain, title, publication_year, file_type, download_path, matched_keywords, matched_section_terms, score, classification. - output/critique_summary.csv: columns doc_id, source_org, score, classification, num_matched_keywords, has_section_terms (true/false), is_official_domain (true/false), is_recent (true/false). - output/review.md: a concise review summarizing which documents are actionable and the reasons (refer only to counts and rule-based checks from docs_metadata.json). - output/search_log.txt: the exact search queries you used and a list of downloaded files with their source domains. Constraints: Use only documents from the official organizations described above; avoid blogs, commercial marketing, or non-official sources. Do not rely on hardcoded facts—derive all judgments from the downloaded files and input/criteria.json. The classification must be computed by the script and must match the actionable_threshold rule in input/criteria.json. Make sure all output paths and file structures are exactly as specified so I can verify them locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
